title: Tenant Relocation Plan, Section 8 Rent Protections, and Community Engagement Demands
summary: Developers detail relocation approach: 30-40 apartments held vacant as staging units; each household relocates for approximately one month while unit is renovated; all moving costs covered by developer. Currently 60 of 200 west campus units already vacant. Model apartment will be renovated first for tours to build enthusiasm. Minimum 30-day advance notice before relocation; developers will assess each household for pets, medical equipment, and mobility needs. Residents who decline renovation cannot be forced but developers will work to convince them. All residents on Section 8 -- pay 30% of income regardless of renovation status; actual rents range $900-$1,900/month with HUD paying balance. If resident loses income, HUD recertification can reduce tenant portion to zero with federal government covering full rent. Council demands community engagement at East Adams/Pioneer Homes standard -- calls Parkside 'second biggest housing transformation in our city.' Council member explains developers are dealing with two distinct communities on the east side despite geographic proximity; homeowners of 30-50 years adjacent to property must also be engaged. Council warns residents deeply skeptical from decades of broken promises by multiple ownership groups: 'they have been told... people have told them the same thing and look things are falling.'

political_signal.description: Council invoking East Adams standard and demanding genuine engagement based on decades of broken promises to Parkside residents

processing_notes: DPD in detected_departments is contextual; actual department is Neighborhood and Business Development
Section 8 = federal Housing Choice Voucher program; HUD recertification = process to adjust tenant rent based on income changes
East Adams/Pioneer Homes used as benchmark for community engagement standard
60 vacant units out of 200 = 30% vacancy rate on west campus pre-renovation
Segment 6 of 7 from Economic Development Committee Meeting, January 22nd 2026
